title: Accessing a Password Manager or Digital Vault
summary: A password manager can be either the easiest shortcut or the hardest lock to pick, depending on planning done in advance.
category: After a Loss III: Estate & Digital
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Accessing a Password Manager or Digital Vault | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A password manager can be either the easiest shortcut or the hardest lock to pick, depending on planning done in advance.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Some password managers have a built-in emergency access or legacy contact feature.
Without advance setup, access after death can be genuinely difficult.
This is a strong argument for setting this up proactively with living family members.

paragraphs: Many modern password managers include an "emergency access" or "legacy contact" feature that can be set up in advance, allowing a designated person to gain access after a waiting period or verification process — worth checking for and enabling now, for any family member's accounts.

Without this kind of advance setup, gaining access after death can be genuinely difficult, sometimes requiring a court order — a strong argument for having this conversation with living family members before it's needed.

nextSteps: Check whether the person's password manager has an emergency access feature already enabled.
If accessible, use it to identify other account credentials.
Set up equivalent legacy access for your own accounts, as a preventive step.
disclaimer: General guidance — specific recovery processes vary by password manager and may require legal assistance if no advance access was set up.
